meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop chasing tutorials and start shipping. Use this expert checklist to master analytics setup using Antigravity and Claude Code for client-ready apps.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop watching and start instrumenting. By integrating robust analytics early, you gain data-driven confidence to ship. This checklist provides a repeatable, automated workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to implement professional event tracking in under 30 minutes, turning your prototypes into production-grade assets.
content.intro:
You are drowning in a sea of 'Build a To-Do App' videos, yet your client work remains stalled at the prototype stage. The screen glows, the code compiles, but you lack the feedback loop to know if your product actually solves a problem. You are stuck in the infinite loop of passive consumption, terrified to hit deploy because you don't know how users will behave.

Relief is here. We are shifting your focus from 'learning' to 'shipping' by automating the most critical post-launch infrastructure: analytics. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, you will transform your development flow into a systematic, data-informed process that impresses clients and proves your value through verifiable metrics.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Identify the three critical actions that define user success in your app to ensure you aren't tracking vanity metrics.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project codebase and suggest 3 high-impact user actions to track that demonstrate product-market fit. Format as a tracking plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate an analytics_plan.md based on my current project feature set'

steps.title: Scaffold the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Use automated scaffolding to inject a clean analytics abstraction layer, keeping your business logic decoupled from the provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust analytics wrapper module that supports PostHog or Mixpanel, using a singleton pattern for easy implementation across the frontend.

steps.title: Automate Event Injection
steps.description: Use AI to identify key event triggers in your existing UI components and inject the tracking calls automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan my components folder and inject the analytics.track() method into all button clicks and form submissions identified in the tracking plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Refactor all interactive components to include automated analytics events'

steps.title: Implement User Identification
steps.description: Ensure every event is tied to a user session to provide meaningful cohort analysis for your clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a user identification middleware that captures unique user IDs upon authentication and maps them to all subsequent analytics events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add user identification middleware to the auth flow'

steps.title: Validation and Testing
steps.description: Verify your implementation by simulating user flows and ensuring events fire correctly in the console.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test script that simulates a full user journey and validates that all analytics events are correctly formatted and transmitted.

pro_tips: Never ship an app without a 'track-all' default; it is easier to filter out noise than to retroactively capture missing data.
Use server-side event forwarding to prevent ad-blockers from killing your data integrity.
Map your analytics events directly to your client's KPIs to secure your next retainer contract.
Automate your analytics reporting by piping raw event data into a Claude-powered summary script every Monday morning.
Always include a 'debug' mode in your analytics wrapper that logs events to the console in development but stays silent in production.
resources: Analytics Best Practices for SaaS: https://analytics-handbook.com
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, focus on building a custom dashboard for your client to visualize the data, then move on to optimizing your conversion funnels.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping with the confidence of a lead engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
